data.title:
Bitcoin exchange inflows are rising as the price drops to ~87K, a seven-month low.

Large deposits (100+ BTC) now make up 45% of all inflows, hitting 7K BTC on Nov 21.

Large holders are increasingly sending BTC to exchanges, reinforcing the current downtrend.

data.title: South Korean financial regulators urge Bithumb to suspend Tether Market services.
data.description: South Korean financial regulators have urged Bithumb to suspend its Tether market service, which allows customers to buy and sell Bitcoin and nine other high-market-cap altcoins using USDT. These 10 assets can still be traded on the Korean Won (KRW) market. Bithumb is currently in beta testing and involves an order book sharing agreement with Stellar. Bithumb stated that due to system maintenance, the service will be shut down at 11:00 AM KRW on November 28th for restructuring to provide a more stable trading environment. (DLNews)

data.title: BlackRock withdrew 953 BTC and 15,722 ETH from Coinbase Prime
data.description: According to Lookonchain, BlackRock withdrew 953 BTC and 15,722 ETH from Coinbase Prime 20 minutes ago.

data.title: Bitcoin News Today: Bitcoin Eyes $89K Short Squeeze as S&P 500 Sits Just 2% Below All-Time High
data.description: Bitcoin held steady near the $87,000 mark during Wednesday’s Wall Street open as analysts warned that a major short squeeze toward $89,000 may be imminent.Despite muted intraday volatility, market positioning shows a buildup of liquidity above spot price — a setup that could force shorts to capitulate if BTC makes a decisive move upward.Liquidity Maps Signal BTC May Be “Pulled Up” Toward $89KData from Cointelegraph Markets Pro and TradingView showed Bitcoin stuck in a narrow range, giving traders time to position aggressively in both directions.With liquidity clusters forming above, analysts expect the market to gravitate toward these liquidation targets.CoinGlass heatmaps highlight the largest pools of liquidations at:$84,500 — downside draw$88,500 — upside drawBTC moving into either zone could trigger cascading liquidations.$89K: The Short Squeeze Line in the SandCrypto investor Ted Pillows called $89,000 the “key reclaim level” that would put shorts under heavy pressure:“If BTC reclaims $89,000, upside liquidity will be swept first.If Bitcoin loses $85,000, the downside liquidity will be taken out before a bounce.”Meanwhile, analyst Lennaert Snyder noted that long/short positioning sat at a nearly perfect split:Long/short ratio: ~50/50$89K remains the near-term resistance wallSnyder expects Bitcoin to flush out stop losses before choosing direction:“Either we gain $89K, or sweep the $80.6K lows and bounce back.”Macro: Bitcoin Flat While the S&P 500 Pushes Toward New HighsU.S. jobless claims came in lower than expected, hinting at improving labor conditions. But unlike stocks, crypto markets barely reacted.The S&P 500 climbed after the opening bell and is now only 2% below its all-time high, according to The Kobeissi Letter:The macro backdrop remains supportive for Bitcoin:Fed Rate Cut Odds SurgeCME’s FedWatch Tool shows:83% probability of a 0.25% rate cut on Dec. 10That’s a dramatic shift from just 30% a week earlier, driven by dovish commentary from key Federal Reserve officials.Rate-cut optimism typically benefits risk assets such as Bitcoin — yet BTC has so far remained directionless as liquidity builds around key resistance.BTC Must Break $89K, or Drop to $80.6K FirstWith long and short traders balanced and liquidity pools tightening, analysts agree Bitcoin is heading toward a decisive move.Bullish trigger:- Reclaiming $89,000 → short squeeze toward $90,000+Bearish trigger:- Losing $85,000 → sweep of $84.5K → potential wick to $80.6K before a reboundFor now, BTC remains range-bound — but not for long. Liquidity pressure suggests a volatility spike is coming, with $89K the level that could unleash the next leg of the move.

data.title: ETH/USDT, BTC/USDT and SOL/USDT Lead Binance USD-M Perpetual Futures Market in Trading Volume Over the Past 24 Hours
data.description: According to Binance Futures data, over the past 24 hours, the ETH/USDT, BTC/USDT, SOL/USDT, ZEC/USDT and MON/USDT trading pairs led the USD-M perpetual futures market in trading volume.ETH/USDT perpetual futures long/short ratio: 2.04, funding rate: 0.0024%.BTC/USDT perpetual futures long/short ratio: 2.44, funding rate: -0.0005%.SOL/USDT perpetual futures long/short ratio: 3.56, funding rate: -0.0085%.ZEC/USDT perpetual futures long/short ratio: 1.00, funding rate: -0.0322%.MON/USDT perpetual futures long/short ratio: 0.97, funding rate: 0.0050%.

data.title: Bloomberg analysts: Nasdaq ISE proposes raising the IBIT options position cap to 1 million contracts.
data.description: Eric Balchunas, a senior ETF analyst at Bloomberg, wrote on the X platform that Nasdaq ISE has proposed raising the option position cap for BlackRock's Bitcoin spot ETF IBIT from 250,000 contracts to 1 million contracts. The previous cap was raised from 25,000 contracts to 250,000 contracts in July 2025.

data.title: Bitcoin Faces Liquidity Stress as Exchange Stablecoins Ratio Hits Record Low
data.description:
According to a new market update shared by CryptoQuant, Bitcoin is entering a period of severe liquidity weakness as the Exchange Stablecoins Ratio on Binance drops to its lowest level in history.
source: https://www.ethnews.com/bitcoin-faces-liquidity-stress-as-exchange-stablecoins-ratio-hits-record-low/

data.title: Portofino Technologies Faces Executive Departures Amidst Internal Changes
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, cryptocurrency market maker Portofino Technologies is experiencing a wave of executive departures following the earlier exit of its Chief Financial Officer and General Counsel. Sources indicate that Chief Revenue Officer Melchior de Villeneuve has recently left the Swiss company. Due to the private nature of the information, the source requested anonymity. Office Affairs Manager Olivia Thurman has confirmed her resignation.

Additionally, two senior developers, Olivier Ravanas and Mike Tryhorn, along with two junior developers, have reportedly departed from the company. LinkedIn records show that De Villeneuve joined Portofino in January of this year, but he has not responded to requests for comment. Ravanas and Tryhorn have also not provided any comments.

As of the time of reporting, Portofino has not responded to multiple email requests for comment.

data.title: Cryptocurrency market maker Portofino Technologies is reportedly experiencing a new wave of employee departures.
data.description: According to sources familiar with the matter, cryptocurrency trading firm Portofino Technologies is facing another wave of executive departures, following the loss of its CFO and general counsel months ago. The sources indicate that Chief Revenue Officer Melchior de Villeneuve recently left the Swiss company, and Head of Office Affairs Olivia Thurman has also confirmed her resignation. Two senior developers, Olivier Ravanas and Mike Tryhorn, along with two junior developers, have also left the crypto market maker firm. (CoinDesk)

data.title: Avail launches on the Nexus mainnet, supporting multiple ecosystems including Base and BNB.
data.description: Modular blockchain platform Avail has launched its cross-chain execution layer Nexus mainnet, supporting multiple ecosystems including Ethereum, BNB Chain, and Base. Nexus employs an intent-driven architecture and multi-source liquidity aggregation, allowing users to seamlessly transfer assets and execute operations across different chains. Unified verification will be achieved through Avail DA in the future. (The Block)

data.title: Tether’s USDT stability score cut to ‘weak’ level as S&P says reserves can’t absorb bitcoin drop
data.description:
S&P flagged gaps in Tether’s disclosures and reserve governance, saying key details on custodians and asset composition remain unclear.
source: https://www.theblock.co/post/380562/tether-usdt-stability-score-weak-sp-reserves-cant-absorb-bitcoin-drop?utm_source=rss&utm_medium=rss

data.title: Swiss Banks Complete Real-Time Settlement Pilot Using Google Cloud’s Universal Ledger
data.description:
Swiss crypto bank AMINA Bank and Deutsche Börse’s Crypto Finance Group have successfully completed a real-time fiat settlement pilot using Google Cloud’s Universal Ledger (GCUL), marking a major step toward modernizing Switzerland’s financial infrastructure.
source: https://www.ethnews.com/swiss-banks-complete-real-time-settlement-pilot-using-google-clouds-universal-ledger/

data.title: S&P Global Ratings Downgrades Tether's USDT Stability
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, S&P Global Ratings has downgraded the stability rating of Tether's USDT stablecoin to the lowest level, citing concerns over its ability to maintain its peg to the U.S. dollar. The agency warned that a decline in Bitcoin prices could lead to under-collateralization risks for the stablecoin.

On Wednesday, analysts at the rating agency lowered USDT's stability rating from 'constrained' to 'weak.' S&P stated that this assessment reflects an increase in exposure to high-risk assets within USDT's reserves over the past year. These assets include Bitcoin, gold, secured loans, and corporate bonds, alongside limited disclosure of information.

data.title: Cryptocurrency's Shift to Institutional Liquidity Highlighted by JPMorgan
data.description:
According to ChainCatcher, JPMorgan has noted a significant transformation in the cryptocurrency landscape, evolving from a "venture capital-style ecosystem" to a "macro asset class supported by institutional liquidity rather than retail speculation."

In its early stages, cryptocurrency projects relied heavily on private funding and lacked liquidity structures, with retail investors often entering the market at high valuations. Currently, retail participation has noticeably decreased, and the market is increasingly dependent on institutional investors to stabilize capital flows, reduce volatility, and anchor long-term pricing.

Despite retaining investment value, cryptocurrencies still face structural inefficiencies and uneven liquidity distribution, leading to significant price fluctuations. Price performance is now more influenced by macroeconomic factors rather than traditional halving cycles. An analyst has suggested that, in the long term, cryptocurrency prices could reach $240,000, viewing it as a sector with potential for growth over several years.

data.title: Ethereum's Gas Fees Determined by Validator Votes, Says Co-Founder
data.description: According to Foresight News, Ethereum co-founder Vitalik Buterin stated that the gas fees on the Ethereum network are set by validator votes, with many validators adopting the recommendations of core developers. However, he emphasized that the inclusion of transactions in blocks is entirely determined by the free market. Buterin highlighted that Ethereum does not follow the approach of Bitcoin's core nodes.

data.title: SOL Treasury company Upexi plans to raise $23 million to strengthen SOL Treasury's strategy.
data.description: Nasdaq-listed Upexi announced a securities purchase agreement with a single institutional investor to issue approximately 3.29 million ordinary shares and an equal number of warrants at a price of $3.04 per share, with an exercise price of $4.00 per warrant, raising a total of $23 million to support its core Solana treasury strategy.

data.title: S&P Global Ratings downgraded Tether's stability assessment to the lowest level.
data.description: S&P Global Ratings downgraded Tether's stability assessment to "5," the weakest level in the rating system, citing an increase in riskier reserve assets. S&P warned that USDT no longer has sufficient buffer to absorb a decline in Bitcoin's value. S&P stated that Bitcoin currently accounts for approximately 5.6% of the total circulating supply of USDT, higher than the approximately 3.9% reserve buffer implied in Tether's latest Q3 financial report released at the end of October. This means that a significant drop in Bitcoin's price, especially coupled with losses in other high-risk assets, could lead to insufficient collateral for USDT. (The Block)

data.title: Texas invests 5 million in strategic Bitcoin reserve
data.description:
Texas invests 5 million USD in a Bitcoin ETF as a first step toward a state crypto reserve and begins operational implementation. The article Texas invests 5 million in strategic Bitcoin reserve was first published on Crypto Valley Journal.
source: https://cryptovalleyjournal.com/hot-topics/news/texas-invests-5-million-in-strategic-bitcoin-reserve/?utm_campaign=rss_reader&utm_medium=rss&utm_source=feed_source&utm_content=Texas+invests+5+million+in+strategic+Bitcoin+reserve

data.title: XRP News Today: : XRP Jumps Over 7% in 24 Hours Amid Strengthening Bullish Momentum
data.description: Key TakeawaysXRP jumped more than 7% in 24 hours, hitting $2.19 as momentum strengthens.New XRP ETFs from Bitwise, Franklin Templeton, Canary and Grayscale generated $85.7M in opening-day volume.Open Interest Delta RSI has entered overheated territory, flashing early reversal risk.Spot–derivatives disconnect suggests speculative leverage is driving the rally.Institutional catalysts and Ripple’s treasury insights could override near-term technical resistance.XRP staged one of the strongest recoveries in the market this week, surging more than 7% in a 24-hour window and reclaiming the $2.19 level. But behind the bullish move, analysts warn that speculative leverage is rapidly building, hinting that XRP may be approaching an overbought zone.The rally follows a massive sentiment shift across crypto after last week’s market-wide crash. But unlike other large-cap assets, XRP’s rebound has been unusually strong, putting the token at the center of renewed institutional and derivatives-driven activity.XRP ETFs Generate $85.7M on Day One, Fueling Institutional DemandThe biggest catalyst behind XRP’s sudden strength is the debut of four XRP exchange-traded funds, now trading on the New York Stock Exchange.Opening-day volume totaled $85.7 million, distributed as follows:Bitwise XRP ETF (XRP) — $36.6MFranklin Templeton XRPZ — $23.6MCanary XRPC — $18.7MGrayscale GXRP — $6.7MBitwise’s fund dominated early flows, highlighting strong institutional preference for its product structure and liquidity profile.These inflows mark one of the strongest ETF launches for an altcoin in 2025, and traders say the new demand is already shaping XRP’s intraday liquidity and volatility patterns.Derivatives Market Overheating — OI Delta RSI Flashes Reversal RiskDespite the bullish ETF boost, on-chain and derivatives signals are flashing caution.CryptoQuant analyst Maartunn reports that the OI Delta RSI — a metric measuring speculative positioning in derivatives — has surged above 70, entering overheated territory.This indicates:A rapid build-up of leveraged long positionsPrice movement driven more by speculation than organic demandGrowing risk of liquidation cascadesA potential local top forming near $2.19Historically, XRP has faced sharp reversals whenever OI Delta RSI enters this range.The takeaway:XRP’s rally is currently fueled more by derivative speculation than spot demand.Spot vs. Derivatives: A Risky Disconnect AppearsAnalysts warn that the growing gap between spot accumulation and leveraged futures trading could become a roadblock for further upside.When derivatives lead price action:Upward moves become fragileSmall corrections can trigger systemic liquidationsBull runs stall below key resistance zonesThis disconnect typically forms before local tops — unless fundamentals shift decisively.Ripple–GTreasury Report Could Become a Major Fundamental CatalystA new joint report from Ripple and GTreasury outlines how digital assets are reshaping global corporate treasury operations, highlighting faster settlement, lower costs, and higher uptime.Although not explicitly promoting XRP, the report underscores XRP’s utility as a bridge currency for institutional payments — a narrative that has historically driven strong inflows during adoption cycles.If institutional awareness grows, this catalyst could:Offset short-term derivative excessBuild real spot-market demandPush XRP through major resistance despite technical headwindsXRP Price Analysis: Key Levels to WatchXRP is currently trading above a critical support cluster:Support$2.18–$2.2738.2% Fibonacci retracement at $2.22Macro support at $1.99–$2.25Price continues to defend these levels, forming higher lows.Resistance$2.69–$2.84 — the primary breakout zoneA daily close above this range would confirm a medium-term trend reversal and open the door to new Fibonacci targets.The ETF inflows improve the odds of a breakout — but only if leveraged froth unwinds cleanly.Conclusion: A Technical–Fundamental Tug of WarXRP currently sits at the intersection of:Bullish Forces$85.7M ETF debut volumeStrong institutional flows led by BitwiseRenewed interest in XRP’s utility from the Ripple–GTreasury reportA broader market recovery following last week’s crashBearish ForcesOverheated derivatives marketOI Delta RSI > 70Disconnect between spot demand and leveraged tradingHistorically elevated risk of reversal after similar setupsThe next major test is the $2.69–$2.84 resistance zone.If ETF demand persists, XRP may break through with momentum.If derivatives unwind first, a retest of support around $2.18–$2.22 becomes likely.Either way, XRP’s volatility is far from over — and ETF flows now play a decisive role in where price moves next.
